basic_details.job_title: Rajasthan Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 111 Anganwadi Worker and Helper Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Women and Child Development (WCD) Department, Barmer district, Rajasthan, has issued Anganwadi Recruitment Notification (Vigyapti Sankhya 06/2026-27) for filling vacant honorary (mandey) posts of Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper in various Child Development Project (Bal Vikas Pariyojana) offices of Barmer district. The notification was issued on 07 July 2026 and invites offline applications from eligible local resident women up to 06 August 2026, 6:00 PM.

important_dates.raw: Notification Issued On: 07 July 2026 Start of Application Submission: Not specifically mentioned in the notification; applications are invited from the date of publication of the notice Last Date to Submit Application: 06 August 2026, till 6:00 PM Last Date to Complete Deficient/Pending Documents: 06 August 2026 (documents cannot be added after this date)

qualification.details: The minimum educational qualification for both Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts is Senior Secondary (Class 12th) pass. Marks/weightage for educational qualification will be determined as per the selection format ("Mulyankan Patra-B") revised on 01.10.2024 issued by the Directorate.

selection_process: Selection will be carried out following the guidelines of the departmental circular dated 12.01.2023 and the amended guidelines dated 01.03.2024 and 01.10.2024

along with other circulars/standards issued by the department from time to time. The selection process is to be conducted through the medium of upcoming Gram Sabhas. Marks for educational qualification of Worker and Helper candidates will be determined as per the Directorate's revised evaluation format ("Mulyankan Patra-B") dated 01.10.2024. Where the Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe / minority population is 50% or more within the coverage area of an Anganwadi centre

selection for that post is mandatorily made from a woman of that respective category. Population figures are determined on the basis of the Anganwadi survey register. The concerned Child Development Project Officer will be held accountable for any irregularity found in the selection process.
how_to_apply: Obtain the application form and check the vacancy details, eligibility conditions and reserved category for your Gram Panchayat/Anganwadi centre from the concerned Bal Vikas Pariyojana (CDPO) office or the official website www.wcd.rajasthan.gov.in.
Fill the application form completely and attach self-attested copies of all required documents.
Prepare two hard copies of the filled application along with self-attested documents.
Submit both hard copies in person at the office of the concerned Bal Vikas Pariyojana Adhikari (CDPO) on working days.
Ensure the application reaches the office on or before 06 August 2026, by 6:00 PM.
If any document is missing at the time of submission, it can be completed by attaching it with a request letter up to the final date of 06 August 2026; documents will not be accepted after this date.
important_instructions: Applications are invited only from local resident women of the Gram Panchayat / revenue village corresponding to each Anganwadi centre — candidates should not apply for centres outside their local Gram Panchayat. Widowed and divorced women can be considered local residents based on either their sasural (marital) or maayka (parental) village. Selection guidelines are governed by departmental circular no. 4244 dated 12.01.2023 and subsequent amendments dated 01.03.2024 and 01.10.2024, along with related orders (e.g., Rajkaj reference no. 10872467 dated 01.10.2024). The notification, along with the Gram Panchayat and revenue village-wise vacancy details, will be displayed on the notice boards of the Zila Parishad, Deputy Director's office, concerned CDPO offices, Panchayat Samiti, Gram Panchayat and Anganwadi centres. The Analyst-cum-Programmer (Deputy Director), Integrated Child Development Services, Rajasthan, Jaipur, has been requested to upload the district's vacancy notification on the departmental website. Any complaint of irregularity in the selection process will be the responsibility of the concerned Bal Vikas Pariyojana Adhikari.

rows.details: Women and Child Development Department, Barmer (Office of the Deputy Director)
Local resident women of the concerned Gram Panchayat / Rajasva Gaon
Offline, at the concerned Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) office

content_sections.heading: Rajasthan Anganwadi Recruitment 2026: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Notification Issued On: 07 July 2026 Start of Application Submission: Not specifically mentioned in the notification; applications are invited from the date of publication of the notice Last Date to Submit Application: 06 August 2026, till 6:00 PM Last Date to Complete Deficient/Pending Documents: 06 August 2026 (documents cannot be added after this date)

content_sections.heading: Barmer Anganwadi Bharti 2026: Project Office-wise Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The vacant posts are spread across 13 Child Development Project (Bal Vikas Pariyojana) offices in Barmer district. Interested women should apply at the project office corresponding to the Gram Panchayat / revenue village where they are local residents. The office-wise vacancy break-up is as follows: In addition to this office-wise summary, the official notification includes a detailed annexure listing 112 individual Anganwadi centres (with AWC code, village panchayat, revenue village and reservation category) spread across the Panchayat Samitis of Baytu, Gudamalani, Sindhri, Gadra Road, Aadel, Barmer Gramin and Sedwa. Because this centre-wise list is highly detailed, candidates are advised to check the official notification PDF or the concerned CDPO office / WCD Rajasthan website for the exact Anganwadi centre and reservation category applicable to their Gram Panchayat.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria for Anganwadi Worker & Helper Recruitment
content_sections.text: Only local resident women of the concerned Gram Panchayat (for rural areas) or the concerned ward of the urban Anganwadi centre (for urban areas) are eligible to apply. A woman applying for an Anganwadi centre should be a local resident of the same Gram Panchayat as the revenue village to which that Anganwadi centre belongs. A widowed or divorced woman may be considered a local resident with reference to either her marital (sasural) home or her parental (maayka) home, as applicable. Both married and unmarried women are eligible to apply for the posts of Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper. If a selected woman marries and, as a result, is no longer a local resident of the concerned Gram Panchayat, her honorary (mandey) service is automatically treated as terminated. Candidates are required to give a self-declaration (affidavit) regarding this at the time of application.

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: The minimum educational qualification for both Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts is Senior Secondary (Class 12th) pass. Marks/weightage for educational qualification will be determined as per the selection format ("Mulyankan Patra-B") revised on 01.10.2024 issued by the Directorate.

rows.category: SC / ST / Widow / Divorced / Special category candidates
rows.age_limit_as_on_date_of_notification: Maximum age relaxed up to 40 years

rows.category: Existing honorary (mandey) workers re-applying for a mandey post

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection will be carried out following the guidelines of the departmental circular dated 12.01.2023 and the amended guidelines dated 01.03.2024 and 01.10.2024, along with other circulars/standards issued by the department from time to time. The selection process is to be conducted through the medium of upcoming Gram Sabhas. Marks for educational qualification of Worker and Helper candidates will be determined as per the Directorate's revised evaluation format ("Mulyankan Patra-B") dated 01.10.2024. Where the Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe / minority population is 50% or more within the coverage area of an Anganwadi centre, selection for that post is mandatorily made from a woman of that respective category. Population figures are determined on the basis of the Anganwadi survey register. The concerned Child Development Project Officer will be held accountable for any irregularity found in the selection process.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Duly filled application form (two self-attested hard copies) Proof of local residency (as per Gram Panchayat / revenue village / ward) Educational qualification certificates (minimum 12th pass) Category certificate, where applicable (SC / ST / OBC, etc.) For OBC (non-creamy layer) candidates: non-creamy layer certificate valid for 3 years from date of issue, as per Social Justice & Empowerment Department circular no. 45955 dated 08.08.2019; a certificate older than 3 years from the notification date will not be considered valid For divorced women: a certified copy of the divorce certificate/decree issued by a competent court, along with a self-declaration that she will not remarry Self-declaration/affidavit regarding automatic termination of mandey service upon change of local residency due to marriage

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Barmer Anganwadi Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Obtain the application form and check the vacancy details, eligibility conditions and reserved category for your Gram Panchayat/Anganwadi centre from the concerned Bal Vikas Pariyojana (CDPO) office or the official website www.wcd.rajasthan.gov.in. Fill the application form completely and attach self-attested copies of all required documents. Prepare two hard copies of the filled application along with self-attested documents. Submit both hard copies in person at the office of the concerned Bal Vikas Pariyojana Adhikari (CDPO) on working days. Ensure the application reaches the office on or before 06 August 2026, by 6:00 PM . If any document is missing at the time of submission, it can be completed by attaching it with a request letter up to the final date of 06 August 2026; documents will not be accepted after this date.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Applications are invited only from local resident women of the Gram Panchayat / revenue village corresponding to each Anganwadi centre — candidates should not apply for centres outside their local Gram Panchayat. Widowed and divorced women can be considered local residents based on either their sasural (marital) or maayka (parental) village. Selection guidelines are governed by departmental circular no. 4244 dated 12.01.2023 and subsequent amendments dated 01.03.2024 and 01.10.2024, along with related orders (e.g., Rajkaj reference no. 10872467 dated 01.10.2024). The notification, along with the Gram Panchayat and revenue village-wise vacancy details, will be displayed on the notice boards of the Zila Parishad, Deputy Director's office, concerned CDPO offices, Panchayat Samiti, Gram Panchayat and Anganwadi centres. The Analyst-cum-Programmer (Deputy Director), Integrated Child Development Services, Rajasthan, Jaipur, has been requested to upload the district's vacancy notification on the departmental website. Any complaint of irregularity in the selection process will be the responsibility of the concerned Bal Vikas Pariyojana Adhikari.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Rajasthan Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 in Barmer?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application for the Barmer Anganwadi Karyakarta and Sahayika recruitment 2026 is 06 August 2026, up to 6:00 PM.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in this Anganwadi recruitment?
faq.answer: There are a total of 111 vacancies, comprising 39 Anganwadi Karyakarta posts and 72 Anganwadi Sahayika posts across 13 project offices in Barmer district.

faq.question: Q3. What is the minimum qualification required for Anganwadi Karyakarta and Sahayika posts?
faq.answer: The minimum educational qualification required for both posts is Class 12th (Senior Secondary) pass.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Anganwadi Bharti 2026?
faq.answer: The general age limit is 18 to 35 years. It is relaxed up to 40 years for SC, ST, widowed, divorced and special category women. Existing honorary mandey workers reapplying for a mandey post have no upper age limit.

faq.question: Q5. Can married women apply for Anganwadi Karyakarta/Sahayika posts?
faq.answer: Yes, both married and unmarried women are eligible to apply. However, if a selected woman marries and thereby stops being a local resident of the concerned Gram Panchayat, her mandey service is automatically considered terminated.

faq.question: Q6. Is there an application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The official notification does not mention any application fee. This information is not available in the notification.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for Rajasthan Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 in Barmer?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application for the Barmer Anganwadi Karyakarta and Sahayika recruitment 2026 is 06 August 2026, up to 6:00 PM.
